memoryArtifacts.headline: Park Hall
memoryArtifacts.description: Descent: Sir John Botetourt, kt. sold 1373 to Sir Henry de Arden (d. c.1400), kt.; to son, Sir Ralph Arden (d. 1420), kt.; to son, Robert de Arden (d. 1452); to son, Walter Arden (d. 1502); to son, Sir John Arden (d. 1526), kt.; to son, Thomas Arden (d. 1563); to grandson, Edward Arden (d. 1583); forfeited to Crown and granted 1586 to Edward Darcy but in 1609 surrendered and returned to Robert Arden (d. 1635); to grandson, Robert Arden (d. 1643); to four sisters, from whom it was bought up by the husband of Godith Arden, Sir Herbert Price (fl. 1664); to son, John Price (fl. 1704); sold 1704 to John Bridgeman of Castle Bromwich Hall, with which it descended thereafter.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Ralph de Arden於大約1290年出生在英國時，他的父親Ralph de Arden20歲，母親Alice Beauchamp16歲。他於大約1338年與Isabel Bromwich在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾卡素 布洛維其結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他去世於1346年之後在英國瓦維其可爾卡素 布洛維其。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ralph de Arden于大约1290年出生在英国时，他的父亲Ralph de Arden20岁，母亲Alice Beauchamp16岁。他于大约1338年与Isabel Bromwich在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾卡素 布洛維其结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。他去世于1346年之后在英国瓦維其可爾卡素 布洛維其。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Arden
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name, either from the district of Arden in Warwickshire or from Arden in Bredbury (Cheshire). The first placename is thought to be derived from a Celtic word meaning ‘high place’, cognate with Ardennes, the name of a forested region on the borders between northeastern France and eastern Belgium. The second derives from Old English hēah ‘high’ + worthign ‘enclosed settlement’.

variants.name: Harden
meanings: English: variant of Arden with prosthetic H-.

English: habitational name, either from Haredene Wood in East Tisbury (Wiltshire), or from Haredon (in Ugborough, Devon). The first placename probably derives from Old English hara ‘hare’ + denu ‘valley’. The second is from Old English hara + dūn ‘hare hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Ralph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

variants.name: Ralf
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Ralph de Arden was born about 1290, in England, his father, Ralph de Arden, was 20 and his mother, Alice Beauchamp, was 16. He married Isabel Bromwich about 1338, in Castle Bromwich, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died after 1346, in Castle Bromwich, Warwickshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ralph de Arden est né environ 1290, à Angleterre, son père, Ralph de Arden, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Alice Beauchamp, avait 16 ans. Il a épousé Isabel Bromwich environ 1338, à Castle Bromwich, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il est décédé après 1346, à Castle Bromwich, Warwickshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Ralph de Ardenが約1290年にイギリスで生まれた時，父Ralph de Ardenは20歳，母Alice Beauchampは16歳であった。約1338年にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアカストル ブロムウィチでIsabel Bromwichと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼は1346年以後にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアカストル ブロムウィチで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Ralph de Arden(이)가 대략 1290년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ralph de Arden(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Beauchamp(은)는 16세였다. 그는 대략 1338년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 카스틀 브롬위치에서 Isabel Bromwich(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1346년 이후 영국 워윅실르 카스틀 브롬위치에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ralph de Arden nasceu aproximadamente 1290, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Ralph de Arden, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Alice Beauchamp, tinha 16 anos. Ele casou-se com Isabel Bromwich aproximadamente 1338, em Castle Bromwich, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ralph de Arden родился около 1290 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени Ralph de Arden было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Beauchamp, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabel Bromwich около 1338 г. в местности кастл бромвич, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он умер после 1346 г. в местности кастл бромвич, варвиккшир, Англия.
